name: Haleiwa Joe's
tagline: North Shore's sit-down seafood spot with a chill surfer vibe and solid fresh fish plates.
mustOrder: Fresh catch of the day — grilled or seared, always local fish
Coconut shrimp — crispy and sweet, a crowd favorite
Joe's salad — mixed greens with macadamia nuts and lilikoi dressing
parking: Parking lot at Haleiwa Town Center, right in front of the restaurant.
talkStory: Opened in 1992 in Haleiwa Town, Haleiwa Joe's became the North Shore's first real sit-down seafood restaurant — before that, your post-surf options were basically food trucks and plate lunch counters. The Haiku Gardens location in Kaneohe eventually closed, making this the sole survivor. Fresh catch is sourced from local harbor boats, and the lilikoi dressing on Joe's salad has a quiet cult following. Sunset on the patio after a North Shore beach day is the move.
